summary: Documentation-only PR adding docs/standards.md as a decomp PR and worker-output review checklist. The new document covers source reconstruction style, matching tactic evidence, data and naming discipline, file/include hygiene, formatting reviewability, and verification expectations. The PR was closed without a recorded merge in the supplied evidence.

key_files.role: New 256-line review checklist for decomp PRs and worker output.
evidence_refs: changed_files: docs/standards.md added=256 deleted=0 hunks=1
raw_diff: new file mode 100644 docs/standards.md
human_pr_text: Add docs/standards.md as a review checklist for decomp PRs and worker output

what_changed.text: Added a new standards document titled Melee PR Review QA Standards.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: # Melee PR Review QA Standards

what_changed.text: Documented source rules for natural loops, typed fields over pointer math, header inlines, project assert/report macros, literals, and canonical control flow/macros.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Source Rules section

what_changed.text: Documented matching tactic rules requiring targeted evidence for tactics such as PAD_STACK, declaration-order changes, dummy locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global/extern access, and temporary padded structs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Require Evidence For Matching Tactics section

what_changed.text: Documented rules to avoid new pragmas, register keyword steering, and inline assembly unless tightly scoped and justified.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Avoid New Pragmas, register, And Inline Assembly section

what_changed.text: Documented data, naming, file, formatting, and verification expectations, including section ownership, TU split evidence, conservative names, truthful headers/includes, reviewable formatting, narrow builds, objdiff/checkdiff, and explicit regression reporting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Data, Naming, And File Rules section
raw_diff: Verification Rules section

smart_moves.text: Separated the standards into do/do-not checklist sections, making the guidance directly usable during PR review.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated Do and Do not sections under each standard

smart_moves.text: Explicitly scoped validation for this PR as documentation-only, reporting git diff whitespace/check validation and not claiming build/test coverage.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Validation - git diff --cached --check; Not run: build/tests (documentation-only change)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated generated-looking blocks should be checked for natural for/while/do-while forms when they vary only by index, pointer, or counter; preserve the original base pointer when later code needs it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Recover Natural Loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prefer typed accesses over raw pointer math: real fields, correct GroundVars/FighterVars/ItemVars union arms, temporary internal structs, then M2C_FIELD, with raw pointer arithmetic as a last resort.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Prefer Typed Fields Over Pointer Math

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded header asserts should be mapped back to owning header inline helpers when identifiable, especially HSD_JObj inline forms from jobj.h line numbers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Recognize Header Inlines

decomp_lessons.lesson: Plain assert/report expansions should use project macros such as HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, or OSPanic when the source behavior matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Use Project Assert And Report Macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not promote constants, strings, or floats into named data symbols without section ownership, symbol metadata, or surrounding-source evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Keep Literals Inline Unless Data Evidence Says Otherwise

decomp_lessons.lesson: Try ordinary C forms such as grouped switch, ternary assignments, ABS, MIN, MAX, CLAMP, and structured control flow before preserving generated branch ladders or gotos.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Use Canonical Control Flow And Macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching tactics should be kept only with targeted build, objdiff, or regression evidence and should not hide real types, shift nearby functions, or change data order.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Require Evidence For Matching Tactics

decomp_lessons.lesson: Verification claims should name narrow touched-object builds, full ninja when blast radius is nontrivial, objdiff/checkdiff for matched symbols or units, and explicit regression reporting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Verification Rules

naming_conventions.convention: Use semantic names only when the role is evidenced.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Be Conservative With Names

naming_conventions.convention: Keep fn_<addr>, lbl_<addr>, or module-address names when semantics are not proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Be Conservative With Names

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj when those roles are clear, and clean up generated temp_rXX-style names once a local role is known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Be Conservative With Names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Require targeted evidence before keeping PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK, declaration-order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy stack locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global/extern access instead of struct paths, or temporary padded structs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Require Evidence For Matching Tactics

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scope unavoidable pragmas tightly and prove pragma or inline-assembly exceptions do not affect adjacent functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Avoid New Pragmas, register, And Inline Assembly

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use data section placement, symbol size/type metadata, local @NNN labels, assert filenames, string clusters, object names, and float groups as matching and TU split evidence rather than compensating with dummy C storage.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Respect Data Sections And TU Splits

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the supplied slice.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: review_comments=0 reviews=0

review_feedback.feedback: Bot report for GALE01 said No changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot], Report for GALE01 ... No changes

follow_up_queries: HSD_JObj jobj.h inline assert line number
M2C_FIELD typed fields GroundVars FighterVars ItemVars
data section ownership literals inline symbols.txt TU split

source_update_candidates.title: PR-authored decomp PR review QA checklist
source_update_candidates.text: Consider the docs/standards.md checklist from PR #2607 as a source candidate for global decomp standards covering natural loops, typed fields, header inlines, assert/report macros, literals/data ownership, canonical control flow, matching-tactic evidence, pragmas/register/inline assembly, data sections/TU splits, conservative naming, truthful headers/includes, formatting reviewability, and verification. Because the PR slice shows the PR closed unmerged and has no human review approval, treat this as a candidate source update only, not accepted policy.

metadata_json: merged_at null
